title: kernel: new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug



**Description**
When I start container I find a kernel message in the logs.
`kernel: new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored`
This message was not present with podman 1.8.2
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run a container using systemd service unit

**Describe the results you received:**
Message in the logs: 
`kernel: new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored`

**Describe the results you expected:**
No message in the logs

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Systemd service unit.
```
[Unit]
Description=System Logging Service
Documentation=https://www.rsyslog.com/doc/
Requires=syslog.socket

[Service]
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/environment
Type=forking
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman rm rsyslogd
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/podman pull our-registry.ourdomain.com/utility/rsyslog-server:latest
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run -d --rm --name rsyslogd --log-driver=journald --log-opt=tag={{.Name}} --env CONTAINER_HOST=%H \
    -v /var/spool/postfix/dev:/var/spool/postfix/dev \
    -v /run/systemd/journal:/run/systemd/journal \
    -v /dev:/dev \
    -v /logs:/usr/local/registrator/logs \
    -v /logs:/logs \
    -v /etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro \
    -v /etc/rsyslog.conf:/etc/rsyslog.conf:ro \
    -v /etc/rsyslog.d:/etc/rsyslog.d:ro \
    -v /var/spool/rsyslog:/var/spool/rsyslog \
    -v /var/log:/var/log \
    --net=host --cap-add=SYSLOG,SYS_NICE --pull=always --systemd=always \
    \
  our-registry.ourdomain.com/utility/rsyslog-server:latest
ExecStop=-/usr/bin/podman stop rsyslogd
ExecStopPost=-/usr/bin/podman rm --force rsyslogd
SyslogIdentifier=rsyslogd
LimitNOFILE=32768
LogLevelMax=6
Restart=always
RestartSec=10s
TimeoutStartSec=120
TimeoutStopSec=15

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

host:

    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.15, commit: '
  cpus: 40
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: host.ourdomain.com
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 5.3.0-45-generic
  memFree: 40666742784
  memTotal: 67154833408
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'cri-o-runc: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/lib/cri-o-runc/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 6446641152
  swapTotal: 6446641152
  uptime: 43h 8m 51.77s (Approximately 1.79 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0

    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay

  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unknown,now 1.9.0~2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: The message is in syslog/dmesg or is it in the joural log for your unit file?

Syslog / dmesg.  It is in /var/log/kern.log file.  It appears on every container restart.

```
2020-04-17T11:52:52.776474-07:00 logserver4n2-la kernel: [213097.320645] new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored
2020-04-17T11:52:52.776474-07:00 logserver4n2-la kernel: [213097.320645] new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored
2020-04-17T11:55:49.513851-07:00 logserver4n2-la kernel: [213273.966375] new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored
```

You are talking about these correct?
[488577.976711] SELinux: mount invalid.  Same superblock, different security settings for (dev mqueue, type mqueue)
[488577.978565] new mount options do not match the existing superblock, will be ignored

They have been around for a very long time.  And we should probably remove them.  I think the container runtimes (crun, runc) are attempting to mount the mqueue with a label, and then when that fails, fall back to mounting the mqueue without the label.

@giuseppe PTAL, do you know if crun has this code?
I run ubuntu and don't have SELinux, so I had no mqueue messages, just similar to  the second one.  My dmesg  is not showing those errors before upgrade to 1.9.0
Are you using crun or runc?  And does this happen in rootless as well as rootful?

I use runc. 
`runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev`
Container run as root.

could you share the `config.json` file that podman generates for the container?

If there is no SELinux we shouldn't specify any mount label to the OCI runtime
I am not sure this is caused by SELinux.  But I am not sure.  I see this as a new kernel message in addition to the original mqueue one.

the error is returned by the kernel when the cgroup v1 flags mismatch the flags specified for the controller if it is already mounted at another location.

I think it is safe to just ignore it
So this is because we are mounting cgroupfs on a container?
I think yes, it is caused by the mount of cgroup v1 inside of the container itself
Did you open a PR in the kernel to stop this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am not sure there is anything to do, AFAICS it is just a warning without any effect
